chemistry.reaction_name: Flow Photo-On-Demand Phosgenation Reactions with Chloroform
chemistry.scope_summary: Gram-scale synthesis of chloroformates, carbonate esters, and polycarbonates using flow photochemical conversion of CHCl3 to COCl2 with yields up to 96%. Applied to primary and secondary alcohols, phenols, and fluorinated alcohols.

optimization_table.key_findings: Entry 3 provided optimal conditions with CHCl3 flow rate 0.25 mmol/min, O2 flow rate 0.23 mmol/min, giving 96% yield of COCl2 with 0.4% unreacted CHCl3

figures.flow_path: syringe pumps → reactor under UV illumination → back pressure regulator → collection vessel
figures.key_information: Photochemical flow reactor setup with UV light source positioned above a coiled reactor, dual syringe pump system for reagent delivery, and back pressure regulation for controlled flow
figures.caption: Scheme 1. Flow On-Demand Phosgenation Reactions by a (a) Conventional Chemical Method with BTC and (b) Photochemical Oxidation of CHCl 3  Developed in this Study | Figure 1.  Flow photoreaction system for photochemical conversion of CHCl 3  to COCl 2 . (1) Flow photoreactor, (2) 40 W low-pressure mercury lamp, (3) mass flow controller, (4) syringe pump, (5) coil heater, (6) T-shaped mixer, (7) back pressure regulator, (8) trap containing alcohol, and (9) trap containing aqueous NaHCO 3 .
Figure 2.  Plot of the yield of COCl 2  vs the exposure time with the flow photoreaction system shown in  Table 1 .

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Chemical phosgenation methods require hazardous phosgene gas or toxic bis(trichloromethyl) carbonate (BTC) reagents
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Flow photochemical oxidation eliminates need for chemical reagents or catalysts, enables continuous production, provides safer operation without phosgene handling
translation_logic.what_changed: Replaced chemical phosgene generation with photochemical oxidation of CHCl3 in flow reactor under UV light
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Final products remain chloroformates, carbonate esters, and polycarbonates
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Eliminates phosgene gas handling and reduces use of toxic BTC reagent

figure_descriptions: Flow photoreaction system schematic showing reactor setup with UV lamp, pumps, and control systems
Plot of COCl2 yield versus exposure time showing optimization results

conflicts: Pass3 reported back_pressure_bar=null but Figure shows BPR present - resolved by setting BPR_present=true from figure
Pass3 reported light position as 'surrounding the reactor tubes' but Figure shows 'above reactor' - resolved using figure information
notes: High quality extraction with clear reactor scheme and optimization data. Some gaps in batch baseline data as this appears to be a flow-only study comparing to literature methods rather than direct batch experiments. Back pressure regulator presence confirmed from figure despite null value in pass3.
